trigger_reason: The investigative process relies on high-level summarization at the expense of evidentiary completeness, creating a 'hallucination-adjacent' workflow where conclusions are stated without supporting technical artifacts.
improvements: Mandate the inclusion of an 'Evidence Source Array' in every report, requiring non-empty pointers to memory dumps, log indices, or network captures.
Implement an 'Evidence Validation' flag for all MITRE ATT&CK mappings, requiring a snippet of the raw log or forensic artifact that triggered the specific technique classification.

Introduce a strict 'Null Analysis' requirement: if lateral movement or persistence are marked 'not found', the report must explicitly state which log sources (e.g., Event ID 4624, 4688) were queried to reach that conclusion.

checklist.note: Reconstruct full chronological sequence using timestamped source logs.
Provide raw log signatures, hashes, or memory artifacts for every asserted finding.
Map to MITRE ATT&CK techniques; each mapping must link to specific evidence provided in the investigation.
Execute memory analysis or state 'N/A' with justification if image was unavailable; define process trees and suspicious hooks.
Audit Event IDs 4624/4648/4672 or equivalent cloud provider logs; verify for non-existence.
Enumerate Run keys, WMI event consumers, and Scheduled Tasks; provide path verification.
Quantify data volume and confirm destination ASN/IP reputation context.
